title: Inactive Azure DevOps Basic seat (90 days): sha256:f86577225f7d8e6e
description:
The user **sha256:f86577225f7d8e6e** holds an **ADO Basic** seat but has recorded no
work-item, code, or pipeline activity in the last 90 days.

Azure DevOps Basic seats are billed per user. Inactive seats can be reclaimed
at no loss of historical data — work items, code, and pipelines remain
accessible.
remediation_steps: 1. Verify the inactivity signal in the Azure DevOps organisation settings (Users > Access levels > Last activity).
2. Contact sha256:f86577225f7d8e6e or their manager to determine if there is a legitimate reason for inactivity (extended leave, role change, cross-project move).
3. If no legitimate reason exists, consider changing the access level from Basic to Stakeholder (free), or removing the user from the organisation.
4. Note: downgrading to Stakeholder limits the user to read-only board access; they cannot create or edit work items in advanced boards, push code, or run pipelines.
verification_checklist: - Inactivity confirmed in ADO Users view for 90+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No open PRs, assigned work items in sprint, or on-call pipeline ownership identified.
- Access level changed to Stakeholder or user removed; decision documented.

title: Azure DevOps parallel jobs over-provisioned: sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2
description:
The Azure DevOps organisation **sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2** owns
**10**
Microsoft-hosted parallel jobs but P95 concurrent usage was only
**3**.

Purchased parallel jobs that regularly go unused represent committed spend that
can be reduced. Consider reducing the purchased count at the next renewal.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the pipeline concurrency report in Azure DevOps organisation settings (Pipelines > Parallel jobs usage).
2. Confirm the P95 concurrent usage trend over 30+ days is consistently below the purchased quantity.
3. Identify whether peak pipeline load is driven by short-lived bursts or sustained concurrency needs.
4. Reduce the purchased parallel job count to match P95 usage plus a small headroom buffer (e.g. P95 + 1 or 2).
5. Monitor pipeline queue times for 30 days after the change to confirm no regression.
verification_checklist: - P95 concurrent usage confirmed below purchased quantity over 30+ days.
- Peak pipeline load pattern reviewed for transient bursts.
- Budget owner approved reduction.
- Parallel job count reduced; queue time monitored for 30 days.

title: ADO user eligible for Stakeholder downgrade: sha256:8b833b79a9a4b66d
description:
The user **sha256:8b833b79a9a4b66d** holds an **ADO Basic** seat but their only observed
activity in the last 90 days is reading boards and commenting. This activity
pattern is fully supported by the free **Stakeholder** access level.

Downgrading to Stakeholder eliminates the per-user Basic seat charge without
reducing the user's actual capability needs.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm in the ADO organisation settings (Users > Access levels) that sha256:8b833b79a9a4b66d's recent activity is limited to board reads and comments.
2. Check whether sha256:8b833b79a9a4b66d has any upcoming sprint tasks, assigned work items requiring editor access, or pipeline ownership.
3. Consult sha256:8b833b79a9a4b66d or their manager to confirm the Stakeholder level covers their workflow.
4. If confirmed, change the access level from Basic to Stakeholder.
5. Inform the user of the change and provide Stakeholder documentation.
verification_checklist: - Only board-read and comment activity confirmed in ADO for 90+ days.
- No upcoming sprint tasks or editor-access requirements identified.
- User or manager consulted and agreed.
- Access level changed to Stakeholder; user notified.

title: ADO Basic+Test Plans seat unused (60 days): sha256:8e3e740e6e0a9abf
description:
The user **sha256:8e3e740e6e0a9abf** holds an **ADO Basic + Test Plans** seat but has not
opened or modified a Test Plan in the last 60 days.

The Test Plans add-on carries a significant per-user premium over the Basic
tier. Users who no longer use Test Plans should be stepped down to Basic.
remediation_steps: 1. Verify in the Azure DevOps organisation settings that sha256:8e3e740e6e0a9abf has no recent Test Plans activity (test runs, test case modifications, test plan creation).
2. Contact sha256:8e3e740e6e0a9abf or their manager to confirm whether Test Plans access is still needed.
3. Check whether sha256:8e3e740e6e0a9abf is the plan owner for any upcoming test cycles.
4. If Test Plans access is no longer needed, change the access level from Basic + Test Plans to Basic.
5. If a future test cycle requires Test Plans, schedule a temporary re-upgrade aligned to the sprint timeline.
verification_checklist: - No Test Plans activity confirmed in ADO for 60+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No upcoming sprint test cycle assigns Test Plans authorship to this user.
- Access level changed to Basic; user notified.

title: Windows VM without Azure Hybrid Benefit: sha256:c0ec52abf9bf4f97
description:
The virtual machine **sha256:c0ec52abf9bf4f97** (SKU: Standard_D4s_v5,
region: eastus) is running Windows
(os_type=Windows) but does not have Azure Hybrid Benefit (AHB) applied
(license_type=None).

Enabling AHB with `license_type=Windows_Server` eliminates the Windows Server
licence cost component from the VM's hourly rate, typically reducing the total
price by 40–50%. This requires the organisation to hold sufficient Windows Server
Software Assurance (SA) or Windows Server subscription licences.

This is an **informational** finding — verify licence entitlement before toggling.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the organisation holds Windows Server Software Assurance or subscription licences that are not already allocated to other VMs. Each two-processor licence covers one D/E-series VM (up to 16 cores) or two A-series VMs.
2. Locate the VM in the Azure portal: Virtual Machines > sha256:c0ec52abf9bf4f97.
3. Under Configuration (or via Azure CLI / ARM template), set the licence type to "Azure Hybrid Benefit for Windows Server".
4. Verify the change in Cost Management: the VM's compute rate should drop to the Linux-equivalent base rate within the next billing cycle.

5. If the organisation does NOT hold sufficient SA licences, do not enable AHB — document this as an intentional exception and consider whether purchasing SA would be cost-effective given the fleet size.
verification_checklist: - Windows Server SA / subscription licence entitlement confirmed: yes / no.
- Sufficient unallocated licences available for this VM's core count: yes / no.
- AHB toggled on for the VM: yes / no.
- Cost reduction verified in the next billing cycle: yes / no.
- If not toggling: rationale documented (e.g., insufficient SA coverage, short-lived VM).

title: Under-utilised reservation with sibling subscription on-demand spend: sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef
description:
The reservation **sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef** is Shared-scope and averaged
**45.0% utilisation**
over the last 30 days, while sibling subscription **sha256:b9e41fceb6ed778d** shows
**$120.0** of on-demand resource spend over the same window.

This indicates a scope-widening opportunity: the reservation has unused capacity,
and a sibling subscription is paying on-demand for workloads that could potentially
fall under the reservation's coverage.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the reservation utilisation details in Azure Cost Management > Reservations for sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef.

2. Identify the on-demand resources in subscription sha256:b9e41fceb6ed778d and verify their SKU family and region match the reservation's coverage (Azure auto-applies reservations on a best-fit basis).
3. Check the reservation's current applied scope (Single, Shared, or ManagementGroup) in the Azure portal.
4. If the sibling subscription's workload is compatible and should be covered, consider widening the reservation's applied scope so the sibling's resources absorb the unused capacity.
5. If the sibling is not compatible or is intentionally excluded, document the rationale.
verification_checklist: - Reservation utilisation trend reviewed in Azure Cost Management for 30+ days.
- Sibling subscription's on-demand resources verified for SKU/region compatibility.
- Reservation applied scope reviewed and documented.
- Scope-widening decision documented with timeline (or exclusion rationale).

The reservation **sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef** is Shared-scope and averaged

3. Check the reservation's current applied scope (Single, Shared, or ManagementGroup) in the Azure portal.

title: Dev/test subscription mismatch: sha256:dd552a8468b582dd
description:
The resource **sha256:dd552a8468b582dd** is tagged
**env=prod** but resides in
subscription **11111111**
(offer: **Dev/Test**).
The environment tag and subscription offer disagree, which may indicate:

- A production workload running in a dev/test subscription (compliance and SLA risk), or
- A dev/test workload in a production subscription (missed pricing discount).
remediation_steps: 1. Verify the environment tag on sha256:dd552a8468b582dd is accurate (check with the workload owner).
2. Verify the subscription offer type is correct for the intended workload class.
3. If a production workload is in a dev/test subscription: plan migration to a production subscription. Dev/test offers may lack SLAs and are not covered by Microsoft's 99.9% uptime commitments.
4. If a dev/test workload is in a production subscription: plan migration to a dev/test subscription to take advantage of discounted pricing.
5. Update the resource tag or subscription placement and document the change.
verification_checklist: - Workload owner confirmed environment classification.
- Subscription offer type verified with subscription admin.
- Mismatch type (prod-in-devtest vs devtest-in-prod) documented.
- Migration plan created or tag corrected, with timeline.

title: Idle virtual machine: sha256:aae8469ec3362fca
description:
The virtual machine **sha256:aae8469ec3362fca** has averaged
**2.0% CPU** and
**8.0 KB/s network**
over the last 14 days. This level of utilisation suggests the VM may be
idle or running a dormant workload.

Deallocating or right-sizing an idle VM eliminates compute and potential
disk-allocation charges.
remediation_steps: 1. Verify the workload owner: check resource tags (Owner, CostCenter, Environment) on sha256:aae8469ec3362fca.
2. Confirm the low-utilisation signal in Azure Monitor metrics (CPU, network, disk IOPS) over 14+ days.
3. Contact the workload owner and ask whether the VM is needed. Provide the utilisation evidence.
4. If the VM is unused: consider deallocating (preserves the disk and config) or deleting (releases all charges). If it is needed but oversized, consider right-sizing to a smaller SKU instead.
5. Schedule a follow-up review in 30 days if the workload owner requests more time.
verification_checklist: - Workload owner identified via resource tags.
- Utilisation data confirmed in Azure Monitor.
- Workload owner consulted and decision documented.
- VM deallocated / deleted / right-sized, or explicit keep decision recorded.

title: Log Analytics workspace on sub-optimal pricing tier: sha256:78d27f318cca56c3
description:
The Log Analytics workspace **sha256:78d27f318cca56c3** ingests an average of
**120.0 GB/day**, which puts it
in the sweet spot for a commitment pricing tier. Moving to
**100gb_per_day_commitment**
could reduce list-price costs by approximately
**32.0%**.

Commitment tiers offer a discounted per-GB rate in exchange for a minimum
daily ingestion commitment. They are billed even on low-ingest days, so
verify the average is representative before switching.
remediation_steps: 1. Review 30-day ingestion trends for sha256:78d27f318cca56c3 in the Azure portal (Log Analytics workspace > Usage and estimated costs).
2. Confirm the average daily ingest volume is stable and representative (not inflated by a one-time event).
3. Calculate the break-even point between pay-as-you-go and 100gb_per_day_commitment.
4. If committed tier is beneficial, change the pricing tier in the workspace settings.
5. Set a calendar reminder to review ingest volume in 90 days.
verification_checklist: - 30-day ingest trend reviewed and confirmed stable.
- Break-even calculation performed.
- No upcoming workload change that would materially reduce ingest volume.
- Tier changed and billing confirmed in next Cost Management report.

title: Oversized virtual machine: sha256:22e77224862f2120
description:
The virtual machine **sha256:22e77224862f2120** (currently **Standard_D8s_v5**) shows
P95 CPU of **25.0%** and
P95 memory of **30.0%** over
14 days. Both metrics remain well below 40%, suggesting the VM is significantly
over-provisioned.

Right-sizing to a smaller SKU in the same family can reduce compute charges
while maintaining adequate headroom.
remediation_steps: 1. Review 14-day CPU and memory percentiles in Azure Monitor for sha256:22e77224862f2120.
2. Identify the recommended target SKU (Standard_D4s_v5) and confirm it is available in the VM's current region.
3. Schedule a maintenance window with the workload owner.
4. Resize the VM and monitor for 48 hours to confirm performance is acceptable.
5. Revert if performance degrades.
verification_checklist: - P95 CPU and memory confirmed below 40% over 14 days in Azure Monitor.
- Target SKU availability confirmed in the VM's region.
- Workload owner approved resize.
- Resize performed and monitored for 48 hours without regression.

title: Unassociated public IP address: sha256:663f944ad18991e7
description:
The Standard public IP address **sha256:663f944ad18991e7** is not associated with any
resource (load balancer, VM NIC, NAT gateway, etc.) and is accruing reservation
charges.

Releasing an unused public IP address removes ongoing charges immediately.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the IP is unassociated in the Azure portal (Networking > Public IP addresses).
2. Check Azure Activity Log and DNS records to determine whether any external system depends on this IP.
3. If no external dependency exists, consider releasing (deleting) the public IP.
4. If the IP is reserved for a future deployment, document the planned use case and timeline.
verification_checklist: - IP confirmed unassociated in Azure portal.
- Activity Log and DNS reviewed for any dependency.
- No active external dependency identified.
- IP released or hold documented with planned use case and review date.

title: Single-scope reservation with sibling on-demand spend: sha256:e8c77c617cd5ec45
description:
The reservation **sha256:e8c77c617cd5ec45** is applied to a single scope
((unknown)) while
sibling subscriptions
((unknown))
carry **$?/mo**
of on-demand spend on potentially compatible workloads.

Widening the reservation scope (Single → Shared or Management Group)
would let sibling workloads absorb unused reserved capacity, reducing
total on-demand cost. However, scope widening changes the chargeback
model — verify that the narrow scope is not required for cost-centre
isolation before making changes.
remediation_steps: 1. Locate the reservation in the Azure portal: Cost Management + Billing > Reservations > sha256:e8c77c617cd5ec45.
2. Review the reservation's current utilisation trend (30-day and 90-day). If utilisation is consistently below 80%, the sibling workloads are strong candidates for absorbing the slack.
3. Identify the sibling subscriptions ((unknown)) and confirm their workloads are compatible with the reserved SKU (GP_Gen5_8).
4. If chargeback isolation is NOT required, widen the reservation's applied scope from Single to Shared (or Management Group if applicable).
5. If chargeback isolation IS required, consider purchasing a separate reservation for the sibling workloads or converting their spend to a Savings Plan.
6. Document the decision and the rationale in the team's FinOps log.
verification_checklist: - Sibling workloads confirmed compatible with reserved SKU: yes / no.
- Chargeback isolation requirement verified with finance: required / not required.
- If widened: scope change applied and new utilisation trend monitored for 7 days.
- If not widened: rationale documented; separate reservation or Savings Plan evaluated.
- Finance / FinOps team notified of the decision and projected cost impact.

title: Under-utilised reservation / savings plan: sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef
description:
The reservation or savings plan **sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef** averaged only
**45.0% utilisation**
over the last 30 days. Reservations below ~80% effective utilisation represent
over-committed spend — you are paying for capacity you are not using.

Exchange or shrink the commitment at the next renewal window to align the
reservation with actual usage.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the reservation utilisation details in Azure Cost Management > Reservations.
2. Identify which resources are (and are not) covered by sha256:0c028d7b5cfb0cef.
3. Determine whether workloads have scaled down or been decommissioned since the reservation was purchased.

4. At the next exchange or renewal window, consider right-sizing the reservation to match current usage (e.g. reduce quantity, change instance size flexibility settings, or switch to a Savings Plan if the workload mix has diversified).
5. If the reservation is not exchangeable, document the residual commitment and its planned end date.
verification_checklist: - Utilisation trend reviewed in Azure Cost Management for 30+ days.
- Root cause of under-utilisation identified (workload change, decommission, etc.).
- Exchange or renewal plan documented with timeline.
- Finance / FinOps team notified of residual commitment.

title: Uncovered Azure spend eligible for Savings Plan: sha256:0b776800ebb56925
description:
The scope **sha256:0b776800ebb56925** has **$1450.0** of uncovered
on-demand spend over **Last30Days**.

Azure's Benefit Recommendations API projects **$180.5**
in savings if you commit to a **P1Y** Savings Plan with an hourly
commit of **$1.85**.

Verify the workload is steady-state and not the trailing edge of a one-off
project, then consider the Savings Plan commitment.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the workload pattern for sha256:0b776800ebb56925 over the past 60 days in Azure Cost Management to confirm steady on-demand spend.
2. Verify this is not a temporary spike or trailing-edge workload (pilot ending, migration rollback, pre-decommission).
3. If the spend is steady-state, evaluate the recommended Savings Plan commitment amount (1.85/hour).
4. Coordinate with finance / procurement on the commitment term (P1Y) and approval process.
5. Submit the Savings Plan commitment via Azure Cost Management or coordinate with your billing administrator.
6. Schedule a 90-day follow-up review to validate actual utilisation and savings achieved.
verification_checklist: - Workload pattern reviewed in Azure Cost Management (60+ days).
- Steady-state spend confirmed (not temporary / pilot / ending workload).
- Finance / procurement approval obtained for the commitment.
- Savings Plan commitment submitted or scheduled.
- 90-day follow-up review scheduled.

title: Unattached managed disk: sha256:9e1ae78edb207307
description:
The managed disk **sha256:9e1ae78edb207307** has been unattached (not mounted to any VM)
for 7 or more days. Unattached disks continue to accrue storage reservation
charges even when no data is being read or written.

Snapshot the disk and delete it if the data is no longer needed, or attach
it to the appropriate VM if detachment was unintentional.
remediation_steps: 1. Identify the disk owner from resource tags (Owner, CostCenter) on sha256:9e1ae78edb207307.
2. Check Azure Activity Log to determine when the disk was last detached and from which VM.
3. Contact the owner and ask whether the disk contents are still needed.
4. If data is no longer needed: consider creating a final snapshot (for recovery), then deleting the disk.
5. If data is still needed but detachment was unintentional: re-attach the disk to the correct VM.
verification_checklist: - Owner identified via resource tags.
- Activity Log reviewed to confirm detach event and duration.
- Owner consulted.
- Disk deleted (with optional snapshot) or re-attached, decision documented.

title: Inactive GitHub Copilot seat (30 days): sha256:57989b1ed0dfd1bf
description:
The user **sha256:57989b1ed0dfd1bf** holds a GitHub Copilot seat but has accepted zero
Copilot suggestions in the last 30 days.

Copilot seats are a per-seat add-on. Seats with no acceptance activity should be
reassigned to developers on the waitlist who are actively writing code.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the zero-acceptance signal in the GitHub Copilot usage dashboard for the organisation.
2. Contact sha256:57989b1ed0dfd1bf or their manager to determine if there is a legitimate reason (role change, extended leave, new to the org).
3. If no legitimate reason exists, consider reassigning the Copilot seat to a developer on the waiting list.
4. Re-evaluate the allocation policy if multiple seats show low acceptance rates.
verification_checklist: - Zero Copilot acceptances confirmed in GitHub usage dashboard for 30+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No legitimate reason for inactivity (leave, role change) identified.
- Seat reassigned or justification documented.

title: GitHub Advanced Security over-provisioned: sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2
description:
GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) is enabled on
**40** repositories
in organisation **sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2** with
**55** unique
committers in the last 90 days, but only
**a subset of**
repositories have produced an alert or scan result.

GHAS is billed per unique active committer. Disabling GHAS on repositories that
produce no scanning signal can reduce the billable committer count.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the GHAS billing breakdown in the GitHub organisation settings (Billing & plans > GitHub Advanced Security).
2. Identify repositories where GHAS is enabled but no code scanning, secret scanning, or Dependabot alerts have fired in 90+ days.
3. For each inactive repository: determine whether GHAS is required by policy (compliance, security baseline).
4. If GHAS is not required by policy on an inactive repo, consider disabling it to reduce billable committers.
5. Re-run the GHAS billing estimate to confirm savings before and after changes.
verification_checklist: - GHAS-enabled repos with no alert history identified.
- Each inactive repo reviewed for policy/compliance requirement.
- Approval obtained from security team before disabling GHAS on any repo.
- GHAS disabled on approved repos; billable committer count reduced.

title: Inactive GitHub seat (90 days): sha256:a0555a9ed6dcbc63
description:
The GitHub user **sha256:a0555a9ed6dcbc63** holds a **GH.ENTERPRISE**
seat but has recorded no commits, pull-request reviews, issue activity, or
sign-ins in the last 90 days.

Inactive seats incur per-seat charges without delivering developer productivity
value. Reclaim the seat or move the user to a free-tier access level if
appropriate.
remediation_steps: 1. Verify the inactivity signal in the GitHub organisation's People view (filter by last activity).
2. Contact sha256:a0555a9ed6dcbc63 or their manager to confirm whether they still require a paid seat.
3. If the user has left the organisation or no longer needs GitHub access: consider removing them from the organisation (releases the seat).
4. If the user needs read-only access (e.g. for viewing repos): outside collaborator access does not consume a paid seat.
5. Document the decision in the GitHub access management record.
verification_checklist: - Inactivity confirmed in GitHub org People view for 90+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No pending code review, open PR, or on-call dependency identified.
- Seat reclaimed or access level changed, decision documented.

title: GitHub runner-minute tier mismatch: sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2
description:
The GitHub organisation **sha256:c4ad5df30840a9d2** consumed
**a mismatched number of**
runner minutes in the last billing period against
**the included**
included minutes.

A significant gap between consumed and included minutes indicates either:
- Over-provisioned plan: you are paying for runner minutes that go unused, or
- Under-provisioned plan: you are regularly exceeding the included quota and incurring overage charges.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the runner-minute usage chart in GitHub organisation Billing & plans.
2. Determine whether usage is consistently above or below the included quota.
3. If consistently over the quota: consider upgrading to a higher tier or switching to self-hosted runners for cost-intensive workflows.
4. If consistently under the quota: consider downgrading to a lower tier at the next billing renewal.
5. Set usage alerts in GitHub billing settings to trigger at 80% of the included quota.
verification_checklist: - Runner-minute usage trend reviewed over 3+ billing periods.
- Consistent over- or under-utilisation confirmed.
- Self-hosted runner feasibility assessed if overages are frequent.
- Tier change planned and approved by budget owner.

title: Copilot for M365 seat inactive 60 days: sha256:7e1324b27dc4326f
description:
The user **sha256:7e1324b27dc4326f** has a **Copilot for M365** add-on seat assigned but
has recorded zero Copilot interactions in the last 60 days.

Copilot seats are expensive add-ons. Inactive seats should be reassigned to
users who will actively use the capability.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the inactivity signal in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center Copilot usage report.
2. Contact sha256:7e1324b27dc4326f or their manager to understand if there is a legitimate reason for non-use (role change, leave, onboarding delay).
3. If no legitimate reason exists, consider reassigning the Copilot seat to an active user on the waitlist.
4. Document the reassignment decision.
verification_checklist: - Copilot usage report reviewed; zero interactions confirmed for 60+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No legitimate reason for inactivity identified.
- Seat reassigned to a user on the waitlist, or documented justification retained.

title: Copilot for M365 seat inactive 60 days: sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc
description:
The user **sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc** has a **Copilot for M365** add-on seat assigned but
has recorded zero Copilot interactions in the last 60 days.

Copilot seats are expensive add-ons. Inactive seats should be reassigned to
users who will actively use the capability.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the inactivity signal in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center Copilot usage report.
2. Contact sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc or their manager to understand if there is a legitimate reason for non-use (role change, leave, onboarding delay).
3. If no legitimate reason exists, consider reassigning the Copilot seat to an active user on the waitlist.
4. Document the reassignment decision.
verification_checklist: - Copilot usage report reviewed; zero interactions confirmed for 60+ days.
- User or manager consulted.
- No legitimate reason for inactivity identified.
- Seat reassigned to a user on the waitlist, or documented justification retained.

title: Disabled account with active license: sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc
description:
The account **sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc** has been disabled (accountEnabled = false) but
still carries **SPE_E3**. Disabled accounts do not consume services,
so the license is accruing charges without delivering value.

Reclaim the license unless a compliance hold or offboarding policy requires
it to remain assigned temporarily.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the account is genuinely disabled and not temporarily suspended (check Azure AD / Entra sign-in logs).
2. Check for compliance holds (eDiscovery, litigation hold, or retention policy) that may require the mailbox to remain licensed.
3. If no hold exists, consider removing SPE_E3 and returning the license to the pool.
4. Archive or soft-delete the account according to your offboarding policy.
verification_checklist: - Account confirmed disabled in Entra ID.
- No active eDiscovery or retention hold on the mailbox.
- License reclaimed and returned to pool.
- Offboarding record updated in HR / ITSM.

title: Disabled account with active license: sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc
description:
The account **sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc** has been disabled (accountEnabled = false) but
still carries **M365_COPILOT**. Disabled accounts do not consume services,
so the license is accruing charges without delivering value.

Reclaim the license unless a compliance hold or offboarding policy requires
it to remain assigned temporarily.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the account is genuinely disabled and not temporarily suspended (check Azure AD / Entra sign-in logs).
2. Check for compliance holds (eDiscovery, litigation hold, or retention policy) that may require the mailbox to remain licensed.
3. If no hold exists, consider removing M365_COPILOT and returning the license to the pool.
4. Archive or soft-delete the account according to your offboarding policy.
verification_checklist: - Account confirmed disabled in Entra ID.
- No active eDiscovery or retention hold on the mailbox.
- License reclaimed and returned to pool.
- Offboarding record updated in HR / ITSM.

title: Duplicate bundle assignment: sha256:f4442878f60fc9cf
description:
The user **sha256:f4442878f60fc9cf** holds **SPE_E3**, which already includes the
capabilities covered by a separately assigned child SKU. Carrying both results
in duplicate charges.

Removing the redundant child SKU eliminates the overlap without affecting any
functionality.
remediation_steps: 1. Identify the specific child SKU (duplicate_sku) that overlaps with SPE_E3.
2. Verify the overlap with the Microsoft 365 SKU documentation or the M365 Maps reference.
3. Consider removing the redundant child SKU from sha256:f4442878f60fc9cf.
4. Confirm the user retains all required functionality after the removal.
verification_checklist: - Overlap confirmed: child SKU capabilities are a strict subset of SPE_E3.
- User or manager consulted and no standalone entitlements depend on the child SKU.
- Removal performed and license returned to pool.
- Change logged in ITSM.

title: E5 license with unused premium features: sha256:e91e727d7a03b1b4
description:
The user **sha256:e91e727d7a03b1b4** is on **SPE_E5** (E5) but none of the
E5-exclusive capabilities — Defender for Office 365 P2, Microsoft Purview DLP /
Information Protection, or Entra ID P2 risk policies — have produced an activity
signal in the last 90 days.

If the E5-exclusive features are genuinely unneeded, stepping down to E3 plus
targeted add-ons may reduce costs significantly.
remediation_steps: 1. Review the Microsoft 365 Admin Center security and compliance reports for sha256:e91e727d7a03b1b4 to confirm absence of Defender P2, Purview, and Entra P2 activity.
2. Check whether the user is subject to a security policy that mandates E5 entitlements (e.g., Conditional Access or tenant-wide security baseline).
3. Consult the security / compliance team before downgrading any user that may be under a compliance or audit scope.
4. If downgrade is approved, consider migrating sha256:e91e727d7a03b1b4 to E3 plus only the add-ons they actually use.
verification_checklist: - E5-exclusive feature usage confirmed absent for 90+ days in Admin Center.
- Security and compliance team consulted.
- No mandatory security baseline requires E5.
- Downgrade path to E3 + targeted add-ons costed and approved.

title: B2B guest with premium M365 license: sha256:a604460dbab73777
description:
The Entra B2B guest **sha256:a604460dbab73777** holds **SPE_E3**, an interactive
M365 user license. Guest users typically rely on the host tenant's resources;
assigning a full user license is unusual and may represent an error or
a misconfigured onboarding.

Verify the assignment is intentional before removing it.
remediation_steps: 1. Contact the business sponsor who invited sha256:a604460dbab73777 and confirm the license assignment is required.
2. Review what M365 services the guest actually needs (Teams chat vs. mailbox, SharePoint edit vs. view, etc.).
3. If the full license is not justified, consider removing SPE_E3 and relying on guest-access entitlements provided by the host tenant.
4. Document the decision in the guest-user lifecycle record.
verification_checklist: - Business sponsor consulted and confirmed (or denied) the license requirement.
- Guest access entitlements reviewed against actual usage.
- License removed or explicitly justified and documented.
- Guest user lifecycle record updated.

title: Over-licensed user vs persona: sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc
description:
The user **sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc** is classified as persona **information_worker** but holds
**SPE_E3**, which provides capabilities beyond what this persona
requires. A lower-cost SKU may be sufficient.

Verify the persona classification is accurate before acting.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm the persona classification for sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc is accurate (check job title, department, and group membership).
2. Review which features sha256:11a193bf7f627bbc actively uses versus what SPE_E3 provides.
3. If the persona classification is correct and the extra features are unused, consider migrating to O365_BUSINESS_PREMIUM.
4. Coordinate the SKU change with the user and their manager to avoid service disruption.
verification_checklist: - Persona classification confirmed accurate.
- User or manager consulted about feature requirements.
- No business-critical workload depends on the extra features in SPE_E3.
- Migration window planned to avoid disruption.

title: Shared mailbox with unnecessary paid license: sha256:65aa6dfa246776b8
description:
The shared mailbox **sha256:65aa6dfa246776b8** is under 50 GB and holds **EXCHANGESTANDARD**.
Shared mailboxes under the 50 GB threshold do not require a paid Exchange Online
license — they can be accessed by delegates without one.

Removing the license eliminates ongoing charges without affecting mailbox
accessibility.
remediation_steps: 1. Confirm sha256:65aa6dfa246776b8 is a shared mailbox (not a user mailbox or resource mailbox) in the Exchange Admin Center.
2. Verify the mailbox size is below 50 GB (9.5 GB reported).
3. Check whether the mailbox has automapping requirements or Outlook profile dependencies that need a license.
4. If no special requirements exist, consider removing EXCHANGESTANDARD from the shared mailbox.
verification_checklist: - Confirmed shared mailbox type in Exchange Admin Center.
- Mailbox size verified below 50 GB.
- No automapping or special licensing requirements identified.
- License removed and returned to pool.

title: Unused M365 license: sha256:a604460dbab73777
description:
The user **sha256:a604460dbab73777** has **SPE_E3** assigned but no service-plan
activity has been detected in the last 30 days.

Inactive licenses accrue charges without delivering value. Verify with the
user's manager and reclaim or downsize the assignment.
remediation_steps: 1. Open the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and review the activity report for sha256:a604460dbab73777.
2. Verify with the user's manager that the license is genuinely unused and not subject to a compliance hold (eDiscovery, litigation hold).
3. If no hold exists, consider removing the SPE_E3 assignment or stepping down to a lower-cost SKU.
4. Return the reclaimed seat to the available pool and update licence tracking records.
verification_checklist: - Confirmed no service-plan activity in M365 Admin Center for 30+ days.
- Manager sign-off obtained before reclaiming.
- No compliance hold (eDiscovery / litigation hold) detected.
- License returned to pool and change logged in ITSM.
